plan_metadata.name: Sample Teams Center Dashboard Remote Debug

steps.description: Click the search input box labeled "Search samples" in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface within Visual Studio Code.
Type "team central dashboard" into the search input field located under the "Samples" tab in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button next to "Team Central Dashboard" in the "Samples" panel of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.Add ${{var:app_name:team-center-dashboard}} in to env.
Click the "Deploy" button under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel within Visual Studio Code to execute the deploy lifecycle stage.
Click the "Deploy" button in the confirmation dialog that asks "Do you want to deploy resources in dev environment?" within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's "Ok to proceed? (y)" exist in terminal log.
Click the "y" key prompt in the terminal of Visual Studio Code to confirm proceeding with the installation of @azure/static-web-apps-cli.
Press Enter key in the VS Code terminal to confirm the prompt "Ok to proceed? (y/n)" during Azure Static Web Apps deployment using the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
@assertion there's notification: "actions in deploy stage executed successfully." exist
Click the "Run and Debug" button in the left sidebar of Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Click the "Debug in Teams (Edge)" button in the Run and Debug panel’s top bar in Visual Studio Code to start a debug session for the current project.
Click the "Launch Remote (Chrome)" option in the Debug configuration dropdown menu in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Launch" dropdown button in the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code to select a debugging configuration.
Click the "Password" input field on the Microsoft login web page to activate it for text entry.
Enter ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the password field on the Microsoft login page for account.
Press Enter after typing the password in the "Enter password" field on the Microsoft login page to submit credentials for user.
Click the "Accept" button on the Microsoft permissions request dialog to grant access for the 'team-central-dashboard-aad' application.
